name: Mailgun API
what_it_does: Developer-focused email sending API with powerful routing, parsing, and validation features for transactional email delivery.
use_cases: Sending transactional emails from agent workflows
Inbound email parsing and processing by agents
Email routing rules and forwarding automation
not_for: Marketing email campaigns at scale (use dedicated marketing tools)
Teams that need a completely free tier indefinitely
Non-technical users needing a drag-and-drop interface
best_when: A developer-focused agent workflow needs transactional email with strong routing, inbound parsing, or email validation capabilities.
avoid_when: You need a large free tier or a polished marketing email UI.

auth.notes: API key + domain-based authentication. Each sending domain has its own API key context. Simple HTTP Basic Auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 5,000 emails/month for the first 3 months only, then paid
pricing.notes: Free trial is time-limited (3 months). After that, requires paid plan. More expensive than SendGrid for high volumes.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages are informative but less structured than SendGrid. HTTP status codes are reliable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Each API call is independent.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier expires after 3 months - unexpected billing for agents in production
Domain verification required before any sending
EU data residency requires explicitly selecting EU region in account settings
Inbound email routing requires MX record changes - not instant

security_score_components.security_notes: Private API key + domain key. Webhook HMAC signing. SOC2 Type II. Domain verification required. Private API key has full account access — domain-specific keys available for isolation.
